divider.chapter2.name: Chapter 2
info.lasercut: Creates a lasercut vector shape, supports corner rounding. Use for rounding dividers with tabs, round stickers, investigator tokens or as a convenient outline for scissors
info.crease: Creates a crease line. Protects a divider from cracks in paint/lacquer and deformation when folding
info.sleeve.customCut.description: Will need to be cut manually from the top of the sleeve
layout.sleeve.outerSleeve.description: Also known as Outer or Character Sleeves. They are made by KMC (CSG Character Sleeve Guard) and Dragon Shield
printInfo.doubleSidedNoBleed.title: Double-sided printing without bleed
printInfo.doubleSidedNoBleed.body: Almost any printer has slight alignment errors, so the image on one side may shift. When cutting a double-sided layout, white stripes can appear or the cut line may go into the divider area.
printInfo.firefoxLargePaper.title: Firefox and paper larger than A4
printInfo.firefoxLargePaper.body: Printing on paper larger than A4 in Firefox looks blurry. For best quality, generate a PDF (the arrow next to the Print button).
printInfo.singleItemPerPage.title: One per page
printInfo.singleItemPerPage.body: Printing in "One per page" mode is available only when generating a PDF.
printInfo.sticker.title: Printing stickers
printInfo.sticker.body: When printing stickers on a regular printer, dense paper may not immediately adhere to the printing roller, and the direction of the paper may deviate. To compensate for this, it is recommended to set the page margins in the settings
credits.author.description:
: You can spend any number
of resources on this address
Remember, {{name}}
is grateful to you
info.bleed: Bleed is needed to remove white borders from the divider edges due to the inevitable error of the knife in 1–2 mm and deviations when printing on both sides
info.sleeves: You can use sleeves instead of laminating a divider. This increases the density of a divider and its lifespan.
info.deckBox: Standard deck boxes and Game Liars for decks. Can be found at Game Genic, Ultimate Guard, Aegis and other
category.3mm.description: The smallest required headroom in the world
divider.layout.arkham-deco.chapter1.desc: Compact dividers for the first chapter campaign box
divider.layout.arkham-deco.deckBox.descr: Dividers for Game Genic, Ultimate Guard, Aegis deck boxes
divider.layout.arkham-deco.chapter2.desc: Compact dividers for the second chapter box
divider.layout.arkham-deco.ucfStandard.d: Dividers for UCF Standard deck boxes from Meeple House
divider.layout.arkham-deco.ucf50.descrip: Dividers for UCF 50 deck boxes from Meeple House
divider.layout.sarnetsky-band.campaign.d: Sarnetsky band divider for encoutner sets and scenarios. For standalone scenarios, use the "Oversize" design variant
divider.layout.sarnetsky-band.standalone: Sarnetsky band divider for standalone scenarios
customStory.translationHelp: Please, help me with the content translation to your language. Contact me using one of the links at the bottom of the page.
contentNotSupported: This content is not supported yet. Please contact the design creator.
